title: New Zealand Squad Named for FIFA World Cup 2026
original_text:
The 26 players who will represent Aotearoa New Zealand at the FIFA World Cup 2026 have been named. Announced today (14 May 2026 NZT) at Eden Park Stadium in Tāmaki Makaurau Auckland, the venue where the team qualified in March 2025, the full squad is: 1: Max Crocombe (22/0) Millwall FC, England

5: Michael Boxall (61/1) Minnesota United. USA

26: Tommy Smith (56/2) Braintree Town, England “Firstly, it is a privilege to be in this role and name a squad for the FIFA World Cup, the pinnacle of the game and the dream of everyone who plays” says All Whites head coach Darren Bazeley. “We have spent the last few years building to this moment and tracking around 55 players in contention to make it on to the plane to Canada, Mexico, the USA. “As a coaching team, we have selected a squad that we feel will put us in the best place to perform, a blend of really exciting young talent and experienced players to maximise our chances of winning games and getting out of our group. “This is never going to be an easy process, and the responsibility isn’t lost on us, but I’m confident we have landed on the best possible squad to face IR Iran, Egypt, Belgium, and beyond. “Now the time for discussions is over, we need to head to the tournament and take the opportunity in front of us to make history for New Zealand.” The All Whites have been drawn in Group G alongside IR Iran (FIFA rank 21), Egypt (FIFA rank 29) and Belgium (FIFA rank 9).  New Zealand starts their FIFA World Cup 2026 journey against IR Iran on Tuesday 16 June, kicking off at 1pm NZT, at Los Angeles Stadium. Next, they face Egypt on Monday 22 June, also kicking off at 1pm NZT, at BC Place in Vancouver. Their final Group G match sees them face the top seed in the pool Belgium on Saturday 27 June, kicking off at 3pm NZT, also at BC Place in Vancouver.  Upcoming Fixtures (NZT): 3 June 2026

Registrations: registrations@nzfootball.co.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
